P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance

Mr. John Bersinger

January 24, 1983 
Statements af Economic Interest 
322-644.4 
Re: 
Our Advice File No. A-82-209 
Dear Mr. Bersinger: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the application 
of Government Code Sections 87400-874051/. 
The facts, as 
presented to me, are that you are a former employee of the 
Department of Social Services (DSS). 
While employed there, you 
participated in the development of a new foster care rate 
setting system for group homes. 
During the development of the 
rate system, there was no consideration of specific group homes, 
nor were there any hearings or other proceedings during which 
the claims of any specific group home were addressed.~7 
You indicated that you would like to begin providing 
professional consulting services to specific group homes on the 
following subjects: 
1. 
Accounting systems; 
2. 
Financial planning; 
!/ Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the 
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 
~/ Your current employment with the Department of 
Education is not connected with group homes or their operation. 
The Department of Social Services will administer the new rate 
setting system. 


Mr. John Bersinger 
January 24, 1983 
Page 2 
3. 
Completion of the forms and schedules required by 
the new rate setting system; 
4. 
Appeals related to the new rate setting system; and 
5. 
Compliance with related State regulations. 
DISCUSSION 
Section 87401 prohibits a former state administrative 
official, after the termination of his employment, from being 
compensated for representing any other person (other than the 
State of California) before any court or state administrative 
agency or officer or employee tnereof: 
a) 
By making any formal or informal appearance, or by 
making any oral or written communication with the intent to 
influence, in connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial 
or other proceeding if: 
b) 
The State of California is a party or has a direct 
and substantial interest; and 
c) 
If 
the proceeding is one in which the former state 
administrative official participated.1f 
In addition, Section 87402 prohibits a former state adminis-
trative official, after the termination of his employment, from 
being compensated for aiding, advising, counselling, consulting 
or assisting in the representation of any other person (except 
the State of California) in any proceeding in which the official 
would be prohibited from appearing under Section 18401. 
The phrase "judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding" 
means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other 
determination .•. or any other particular matter involving a 
specific party or parties in any court or state administrative 
agency •.•• " 
(Section 87400 (c).) 
This definition limits the 
application of Sections 87401 and 87402 to situations where the 
state employee worked on a governmental matter affecting a 
specific party and now plans to work privately for the same 
party on the same matter. 
In your case, you worked on 
1f See Section 87400 for the definitions of "state 
administrative agency"; "state administrative official"; 
and 
"participated" . 


Mr. John Bersinger 
January 24, 1983 
Page 3 
governmental matters in which there was no involvement of any 
specific group home. 
Therefore, you are not prohibited from 
engaging in the activities that you listed. 
I hope that I have clarified this matter for you. 
Please 
note that this advice is based solely on the information that 
you provided me with in you letter. 
If the facts are different 
than you have indicated, the immunity granted by this advice 
letter (see Section 83ll4(b» 
will not apply. 
If you have any 
additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 
916/322-5901. 
JSM: km 
Very truly yours, 
~~

John Bersinger 
4609 Mulford Ave. 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95821 
December 14, 1982 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95804 
Attn: 
Barbara Hilman 
This letter is to request a written opinion as to the applicability 
of Government Code Section 87400 to the following outside employment 
activities which I am now considering: 
Sepcifically, I am a former employee of the Department of Social 
Services (DSS), now employed by the Department of Education (SDE). 
While employed by DSS (1980-82), I participated in the development 
of a new foster care rate setting system for group homes as a Staff 
Services Manager I in the Foster Care Rates Project. 
During the de-
velopment of the new rate setting system, there was never any involve-
ment or consideration of specific group homes, nor was there any hear-
ings or other proceedings during which the claims of any group home 
were addressed. 
My current employment with SDE is in no way connected 
with group homes or their operation 
For the future, I am considering providing professional consulting 
services to specific group homes in any or all of the following areas: 
1) 
Accounting systems 
2) 
Financial Planning 
3) 
Completion of forms and schedules 
(required by the new rate setting system) 
4) 
Appeals relating to the new rate setting system 
5) 
Compliance with related State regulations 
(It is currently planned that the new rate setting system will be 
directly administered by DSS, beginning in 1983.) 
Having already discussed this matter verbally with Janice McLean of 
your staff, I would appreciate a written response at your earliest 
convenience. 
John Bersin

SACRAMENTO OF'FlCS 
SUITE 550,1225 EiGHTH STREET 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95614 
(916) 446-6752 
Re: 
Reporting Nonmonetary "Late" Contributions 
Dear Jeannie: 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of December 1, 
1982, I am asking that you confirm in writing that it is 
permissible for filers who anticipate nonmonetary contri-
butions of $1,000 or more during a late filing period to 
estimate the value of the nonmonetary contributions once for 
the entire late filing period and file one late contribution 
notice. 
The one notice would cover the period for late 
contribution notification. 
Please direct a copy of your response to me to the 
Political Reform Division of the Secretary of State's office 
so that they may be apprised of the Commission's advice in 
this matter. 
Thank you for your prompt attent¥on to this matter.

sections.question: Whether Government Code Sections 87400-87405 prohibit a former DSS employee from providing professional consulting services to specific group homes in the areas of: (1) Accounting systems; (2) Financial planning; (3) Completion of forms and schedules required by the new rate setting system; (4) Appeals related to the new rate setting system; and (5) Compliance with related State regulations. The former employee participated in development of a new foster care rate setting system for group homes while employed by DSS (1980-82), but the development involved no consideration of specific group homes and no hearings or proceedings addressing specific group home claims.
sections.conclusion: In your case, you worked on governmental matters in which there was no involvement of any specific group home. Therefore, you are not prohibited from engaging in the activities that you listed.

and substantial interest; and

the proceeding is one in which the former state 
administrative official participated.1f

trative official, after the termination of his employment, from 
being compensated for aiding, advising, counselling, consulting 
or assisting in the representation of any other person (except

would be prohibited from appearing under Section 18401. 
The phrase "judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding" 
means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other

specific party or parties in any court or state administrative 
agency •.•• " 
(Section 87400 (c).) 
This definition limits the 
application of Sections 87401 and 87402 to situations where the 
state employee worked on a governmental matter affecting a 
specific party and now plans to work privately for the same 
party on the same matter.

and

governmental matters in which there was no involvement of any 
specific group home. 
Therefore, you are not prohibited from 
engaging in the activities that you listed. 
I hope that I have clarified this matter for you.

note that this advice is based solely on the information that 
you provided me with in you letter.

than you have indicated, the immunity granted by this advice

sections.question_synthetic: Is a former DSS employee who participated in developing a foster care rate setting system (without involvement of specific group homes) prohibited from providing consulting services to specific group homes on accounting, financial planning, forms completion, appeals, and regulatory compliance related to that system?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The former employee is not prohibited from providing the proposed consulting services because Section 87401-87402 restrictions apply only when a state employee worked on a governmental matter affecting a specific party and now plans to work privately for the same party on the same matter. Since the rate setting system development involved no specific group homes, these restrictions do not apply.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document contains multiple related items: the original incoming request letter (Dec 14, 1982), a brief forwarding letter, and the main FPPC advice response (Jan 24, 1983). Also includes an unrelated letter from law firm regarding nonmonetary contributions. Extraction focused on the primary advice letter A-82-209. High confidence in question and conclusion sections based on clear DISCUSSION and statutory analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Government Code Sections 87400-87405 prohibit a former DSS employee from providing professional consulting services to specific group homes in the areas of: (1) Accounting systems; (2) Financial planning; (3) Completion of forms and schedules required by the new rate setting system; (4) Appeals related to the new rate setting system; and (5) Compliance with related State regulations. The former employee participated in development of a new foster care rate setting system for group homes while employed by DSS (1980-82), but the development involved no consideration of specific group homes and no hearings or proceedings addressing specific group home claims.

CONCLUSION: In your case, you worked on governmental matters in which there was no involvement of any specific group home. Therefore, you are not prohibited from engaging in the activities that you listed.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-82-209 addresses whether a former Department of Social Services employee can provide consulting services to specific group homes related to a foster care rate setting system he helped develop. The FPPC concludes the employee is not prohibited from such consulting work because his prior governmental work on the rate system did not involve any specific group homes.
